name: Jessica O'Connell, EMILY's List
subject: What we know about Trump's speech >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7c7a/24c69fd9/f6b10456/18e0eea4/3848726216/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all&source=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all")
---

Peter,

Tonight is President Trump's first joint address to Congress.

We don't know exactly what he'll touch on, but here's what's likely: repealing Obamacare, next steps on the illegal Muslim ban, building a wall between the U.S. and Mexico, cuts to diplomacy programs that help keep us safe, and tax cuts for his wealthy friends.

One thing we absolutely know will happen: It will be clearer than ever that we need strong pro-choice Democratic women to push back against these dangerous policies.

So we need to buckle down and make sure we hit our End-of-Month fundraising goal of $125,000 -- we're just $22,138 short.

**[Donate $3 before midnight to reject the Trump agenda and elect champions who will fight back against the GOP.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7c7a/24c69fd9/f6b10456/18e0eea5/3848726216/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170225_email_EOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+before+midnight+to+reject+the+T&utm_campaign=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all&source=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all")**

Trump's vision of America is one full of divisiveness that I refuse to accept. We've seen the kind of progress we can make when we all work together. From Nevada Senator Catherine Cortez Masto's historic victory as our first Latina senator to Gabrielle Gifford's inspiring activism to several of our congressional women starting a new caucus for immigrants, EMILY's List women are fighting to make positive change.

That's the America that Trump wants to marginalize. And it's what we fight for at EMILY's List every day.

**We're facing an End-of-Month deadline at midnight. Donate $3 now to help keep up the fight:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Feb-End-of-Month](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7c7a/24c69fd9/f6b10456/18e0eea6/3848726216/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170225_email_EOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgFebEndofMonth&utm_campaign=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all&source=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all")**

Thanks,

Jess O'Connell
Executive Director, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7c7a/24c69fd9/f6b10456/18e0eea7/3848726216/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170225_email_EOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all&source=WQEFE231N_digital_email_20170228_TrumpSpeech_n-all")

subject: Wall Street Donald attacks working people
body:
Tell Congressional Democrats: Block low-wage “right-to-work” laws (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

Petition to Congressional Democrats:
"Block and resist low-wage ‘right-to-work’ laws that drag down the wages and benefits of all working people, unionized or not."

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

When Republicans help Wall Street attack unions, we all end up poorer. But now that radical conservatives and financiers have a friendly puppet in the White House, they are planning a national assault on unions.1

The right-wing American Legislative Exchange Council and big corporations have been pushing low-wage work laws across the country for years. These so-called “right to work” laws let people who have good-paying jobs because of unions weasel out of paying union dues, pitting workers against each other and giving Wall Street far more power. Now, House Republicans led by right-wing extremist Steve King have introduced a new, national low-wage “right-to-work” bill, and they have an ally in Donald Trump.2

Too many Democrats have abandoned unions for far too long. We need to force them to find their spines and fiercely oppose this disastrous legislation.

Tell Congressional Democrats: Block and resist low-wage “right-to-work” laws. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

Since seizing power in 2010, Republicans in state legislatures have stood side-by-side with Wall Street to mount attack after attack on unions. Now, 2017 is poised to be a disastrous year for working people. Two states have already adopted low-wage work laws, and more could follow. Worst of all, Republicans in the House have proposed a national law – and for the first time they have an ally in the White House. Trump said during the campaign he supports such laws, and his press secretary recently confirmed it.3,4

Trump’s plan appears to be to drag American workers down to the level of their global counterparts and race China to the bottom – no surprise from a man who makes many of his products overseas.5

Low-wage work laws hurt everyone. In states with these laws, working people make $6,000 less per year, regardless of whether they are in a union or not. Income inequality is higher. And fewer people have access to the benefits that unions fight for, like health care and pensions. Unions check unlimited corporate power and bring balance to the economy, making everyone better off.6

Tell Congressional Democrats: Block and resist low-wage “right-to-work” laws. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

The same people pushing these low-wage work laws routinely fight against worker safety, the right to organize and higher wages. They are a sneak attack to give international corporations more power by undermining the unions that dare to stand up to them.7 Now Trump is poised to help his wealthy pals by attacking working people, including some of the people who voted for him.

For the sake of their party and the country, Democrats need to unequivocally side with working people against low-wage work laws. We need to make sure they get the message.

Tell Congressional Democrats: Block and resist low-wage “right-to-work” laws. Click below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/ (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

Thank you for speaking out,

Murshed Zaheed, Political Director
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_right_to_work_dems/)

Bryce Covert, “House Republicans push forward anti-union ‘right-to-work’ bill (https://thinkprogress.org/right-to-work-king-wilson-569656534d4b#.6wzwx74xi) ,” ThinkProgress, Feb. 1, 2017. Ibid. Ibid. Berry Craig, “Spicer: Trump 'believes in right to work' (https://www.dailykos.com/story/2017/2/4/1629973/-Spicer-Trump-believes-in-right-to-work) ,” DailyKos, Feb. 4, 2017. Michael Hayne, “Here Are All of the Trump Products Made Overseas (http://www.alternet.org/election-2016/here-are-all-trump-products-made-overseas) ,” AlterNet, Oct. 11, 2016. Covert, “House Republicans push forward anti-union ‘right-to-work’ bill (https://thinkprogress.org/right-to-work-king-wilson-569656534d4b#.6wzwx74xi) .” Ibid.

name: Patrick Murphy
subject: The time is now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by PEM PAC | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida
body:
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e31/738133e4/330d2cf3/7f85b767/3225556171/VEsH/ "https://www.murphyforflorida.com")
---
| Friends,

President Trump's address last night was short on substance and long on empty rhetoric.

I wrote an **[op-ed in the _Tampa Bay Times_ today](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e31/738133e4/330d2cf3/7f85b760/3225556171/VEsE/ "http://www.tampabay.com/opinion/columns/column-standing-up-to-trump/2314837")** about the urgent need for Congress to reign in Trump's harmful policies and ideology. It read, in part:
Standing up to the President comes at a high cost. If you're a department store, it could mean a Twitter shaming and boycotts. If you're a federal judge, it could mean being publicly scorned alongside the entire judicial branch and system of checks and balances. If you're a reporter, it could mean being labeled an enemy of the American people.

But with Republicans now in control of the Presidency, Senate, and House of Representatives, millions of Americans who voted for someone other than Donald Trump - the 54% of us - are waiting for someone to stand up to the President and his reckless impulses.

In my bid for U.S. Senate last year against Marco Rubio, deference to Trump was a topic that came up often. It dominated our first debate. Sen. Rubio told an audience in Orlando that night - and voters across Florida - that "the job of the U.S. Senate is not to blindly trust the President because they happen to be from your own party... Of the two of us on this stage tonight, there is only one person who, if I go to the Senate, will stand up to the next President of the United States."

Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, who misled a Senate panel about lobbying against Russia sanctions? Confirmed with every Republican vote. Education Secretary Betsy DeVos, who said we needed guns in schools to protect our kids from grizzly bears? Confirmed. Energy secretary nominee Rick Perry, who didn't even know what his department did when he accepted the job? He'll probably be confirmed shortly without a break in the ranks.

If there was ever a time to put country before party, it is now.

President Obama was routinely criticized by his political opponents throughout his two terms for partisanship and overreach. A rightfully concerned America asks, when does the standing up to Trump begin?
I truly believe that time is now. To read the whole piece, please check it out [**over here at the _Tampa Bay Times_**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e31/738133e4/330d2cf3/7f85b760/3225556171/VEsF/ "http://www.tampabay.com/opinion/columns/column-standing-up-to-trump/2314837").

name: Shannon Jackson, Our Revolution
subject: Our response to Trump's address
body:
Mr. Trump, this is what a REAL agenda for working families looks like.
#
Sisters and Brothers,
Last night, Mr. Trump tried to trick us into believing he cares about the needs of middle income and working families all across the country. But Trump's economic team is shamefully comprised of corporate elites from Goldman Sachs and other Wall Street firms who helped destroy our economy less than ten years ago. We will not allow these corporate bandits to loot the wealth of America's families again.
We believe that higher wages, protecting Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security, and ensuring that our trade policies keep manufacturing jobs here in America are essential to supporting working Americans in every zip code. [**This agenda -- a progressive agenda -- is what we are fighting for.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0a79/43f17890/21e2bb5c/62002a30/814494004/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/endorse-our-progressive-agenda?source=em170301-full")
Contrast this with Trump's destructive agenda of tax breaks for the wealthy and corporations, increased military spending, environmental degradation and gutting aid to public schools. His plan to enrich his friends and business partners at our expense couldn't be more clear.
[**Endorse our progressive agenda to show Trump and his special interest friends that we are more powerful than the corporate executives and lobbyists in his Cabinet.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0a79/43f17890/21e2bb5c/62002a30/814494004/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/endorse-our-progressive-agenda?source=em170301-full")
Wall Street, the fossil fuel industry and other high dollar campaign donors do not belong in the White House.
We face many crises in America today. Tens of millions of people living in poverty, a broken campaign finance system, ever-rising costs in education, housing and child care, and families torn apart by aggressive deportations. Let Trump know that an attack on any of us is an attack on all of us.
[**Add your voice to show this administration where our priorities are -- with a progressive agenda that protects working families and vulnerable communities who are under attack.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0a79/43f17890/21e2bb5c/62002a30/814494004/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/endorse-our-progressive-agenda?source=em170301-full")
The need for a political revolution has never been greater.
In solidarity,
Shannon Jackson
Executive Director
Our Revolution

name: Jill Vogel for Virginia
subject: Jill Vogel Wins Virginia Beach Tea Party Candidate Forum
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Virginia45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
Republican momentum continues to build behind Jill Vogel
---
| | | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
March 1st, 2017

VIRGINIA BEACH, VIRGINIA - Following the conclusion of this evening's debate among Republican candidates for Lieutenant Governor, Senator Jill Vogel released the following statement celebrating her debate victory and the audience's enthusiastic reception of her positive vision and unequaled record of action on the most important issues in the race."I would like to thank all of our hosts this evening for another outstanding opportunity to share my positive vision for conservative reform in Virginia," said Senator Jill Vogel. "I am glad we had this opportunity to examine in detail some of the most important issues and policies in this race."Before a packed audience in excess of 250, Vogel drew tremendous applause by remaining positive and committed to the core issues of the race"Virginia must embrace conservative reforms on reducing regulations and lowering taxes to revive our sluggish economy," added Vogel. "I am running because I know Virginia's economy can do better than 48th in growth."Throughout the debate, Vogel highlighted the key legislative accomplishments at the center of her pro-growth platform, including her leadership in passing Virginia's Regulatory Rollback Amendment and introducing a tax reform plan calling for refunds of budget surpluses and automatic reductions of tax rates during years of strong revenue growth."Jill Vogel clearly won this debate by offering the most substantive vision for returning Virginia to economic growth through regulatory reform and tax cuts," noted Campaign Manager Pat Trueman. "Jill's conservative record is unequaled in this race. With each passing day, Virginia Republicans continue unifying behind Jill because they see in her a positive candidate with the best conservative record and the best running mate for the entire GOP ticket."Thus far in the race, Vogel's positive and issue-focused campaign has set itself apart from the field in remaining laser-focused on Jill's conservative policies and record. The result is predictable: Jill has raised more money than all of her opponents while posting key, field-leading endorsement totals among local Republican leaders, including [Commonwealth's Attorneys](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ACA9ZdGeo8tgpktmRETDCB_GSQPgFhc1WUuql6XOx_ISgHj4KV4927jXg0p1rDsoPOT9j2yyYnFs6BL1epo_sh4bB00OO1W_55JautWpEcJb8OPeDvniqFxx545rcziqOL5Pmvg0Pfk2qae6J0gqfSP2tvczmeKQPw28F69WB61bQaUReHxDYe1hU_A71PLegbIqzaHVYrBpTP5U6PZNB7OYT7TbUyRMiNrc8KMLWgMFTNsZYp2R5l9aC_ZrPU2E25rr98-T-bM=&c=dnxk3f5bmLfZ-35fbFwZsrRW3bdVM060gi7_eNLnBl1ZMdz2Zb8wIg==&ch=klORzb15BBrF6PwmDpUNYlf5COb0_m163UK4ye1K1BtHvEJStfIzKg==), as well as [local Sheriffs](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ACA9ZdGeo8tgpktmRETDCB_GSQPgFhc1WUuql6XOx_ISgHj4KV492wb9L2TbbtxkkTUFynPz8pjL8-SIP5yHqaFspD5TYrFpLHcW53lyHp2ZtZtfUHiVViFC8cQTQOWl06USmJTkhac0E_ElPd9RVdnvOwnUgPdt26j0J9lGw1kBGPDmU8kINLownGOxQZfOwuYZqXsUew9o9PJr5wHpHfO2gCtWemtgy5Gyi3nPImI1LWTYjfuEh3HIgnbylE-1R3vHBQeA2hA=&c=dnxk3f5bmLfZ-35fbFwZsrRW3bdVM060gi7_eNLnBl1ZMdz2Zb8wIg==&ch=klORzb15BBrF6PwmDpUNYlf5COb0_m163UK4ye1K1BtHvEJStfIzKg==).###

P. S., You can celebrate Jill's win tonight by [ordering your free Jill Vogel for Lieutenant Governor bumper stickers on our campaign website](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ACA9ZdGeo8tgpktmRETDCB_GSQPgFhc1WUuql6XOx_ISgHj4KV4925akyLHzVt0oV_BnrMtIChHh68-rdPMbVsad0t42ig5sUdSQo4xYrV4bezxg734gmMtlncU1lK6yGWIqZMWUVRY5dogkWqodfvh_7EBosIHX8bMbM9xWTNStfM6LxCm5ATl0kKwmE38otsaBsT3ATNwaOlEcxm94Ug==&c=dnxk3f5bmLfZ-35fbFwZsrRW3bdVM060gi7_eNLnBl1ZMdz2Zb8wIg==&ch=klORzb15BBrF6PwmDpUNYlf5COb0_m163UK4ye1K1BtHvEJStfIzKg==). Hello,
Last night, Trump's first address to a joint session of Congress made clear once again that he will never acknowledge the true dangers of his agenda.
He talked about repealing the Affordable Care Act, which would deeply harm our efforts to combat the substance abuse crisis.
He discussed his reckless federal budget outline that would gravely hurt our middle-class families and economy, making drastic cuts to domestic priorities without making us any safer.
We deserve a President who will work to improve the lives of hard-working Americans, not one who pushes a partisan agenda that puts corporate special interests before middle class families.
[**If Trump's address did little to ease your anxiety about the direction he is taking our country, add your name to tell President Trump that you won't stand for a reckless agenda.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd1a/2a380d31/15f36521/feec43/3581217081/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/no-reckless-agenda?source=e20170301_TrumpAddress&firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you for speaking out,
Team Maggie
